pattern: Adding the integer digit-by-digit into the array from the least significant end propagates a carry the way grade-school addition does, growing the result only when the carry survives the leading digit.
hints: Treat the integer's value as something you fold into the array starting from its rightmost digit.
At each position add the current array digit plus whatever remains of the number, keep the ones place, carry the rest.

Iterate from the last index: total = num[i] + (k % 10... or simply add k into a carry); push total % 10, carry = total // 10. After the array is consumed keep splitting the leftover carry into digits, then reverse.

edge_inputs.description: result is longer than the input array
all nines force a brand new leading digit
single nine plus one rolls to ten
k much larger than the array value

stress.description: long random digit array plus a random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 8000))
    num = [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(n)]
    if num[0] == 0 and n > 1:
        num[0] = rng.randint(1, 9)
    k = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    return [num, k]
stress.note: no leading zero unless the array is a lone zero
